commands.review_summary:
The branch annotates successful current-source `message(action="send")` calls as delivered source replies and uses that evidence to suppress later private final payloads without replaying the message-tool reply.

PR surface: Source +122, Tests +75. Total +197 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main only uses source-reply payloads for `message_tool_only` suppression, while the reported automatic-mode path can still surface a later private final. I did not run a live Telegram repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor proof and maintainer delivery-path review rather than an automated repair, since no narrow code defect was found.

target.title: fix(agents): suppress private finals after message tool source replies
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91330.

This treats successful `message(action="send")` calls to the current source conversation as delivered source replies. When the agent later emits private bookkeeping text, the final payload builder now suppresses that private final instead of surfacing it as the channel-visible conclusion.

## Real incident this fixes

We hit this on a Windows/OpenClaw node (`2026.6.5-beta.2`) while diagnosing QQBot setup from a Telegram direct chat:

- First rollout was interrupted by an in-chat `openclaw gateway restart` before any final answer. That remains adjacent to restart/session-continuation work.
- Second rollout completed and sent the real diagnostic conclusion through the `message` tool to the same Telegram source conversation.
- The model then ended with private bookkeeping text (`已通过 Telegram 回复，并把 QQBot 安装状态记到今天的 memory。`), which became the visible final surface and made the turn look incomplete.

The actual diagnosis was that QQBot was not broken; plugin/config skeleton existed, Gateway/config were healthy, and the blocker was missing QQ Open Platform credentials. The UX bug was the delivered message-tool conclusion being obscured by the private final.

## What changed

- Annotate successful current-source `send` results with source-reply metadata.
  - Works for implicit current-source sends.
  - Also handles current-source sends where the route/session key resolves to the active session.
  - Does **not** annotate sends routed to another target/session.
- Payload building now distinguishes:
  - `message_tool_only`: keep existing source-reply mirror payload behavior.
  - `automatic`: use delivered source-reply evidence only to suppress later private assistant artifacts; do not resend the message-tool reply.

## Related context

Adjacent but not sufficient on their own:

- #90610 — final-answer candidate visibility; related final-selection UX, but not message-tool-delivered source replies followed by private bookkeeping finals.
- #88992, #90095, #84485, #85235 — adjacent message-tool / delivery UX changes.
- #57425, #60864, #82772, #47025 — related restart/session-continuation work. This PR intentionally does not attempt the broader self-restart recovery fix.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
